content:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron's got this lean, angular look — sharp cheekbones, that scar through his eyebrow, always slightly disheveled in a way that suggests he forgot to care rather than tried not to. He's a talented writer — genuinely talented, the kind who makes you feel something with a sentence. He's perceptive, principled, funny when he lets his guard down, which isn't often enough. He's my closest friend here besides Katie — the guy on the other side of the wall, the one I talk to at 1 AM. I think he sees me as a good friend, maybe someone he worries about. He told me about his father's dementia after I overheard the call — the only personal thing he's ever volunteered. I know his novel is consuming him. I suspect he has feelings for Katie but he's never said it and he's never acted on it. That restraint is why I trust him.
summary:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron's got this lean, angular look — sharp cheekbones, that scar through his eyebrow, always slightly disheveled in a way that suggests he forgot to care rather than tried not to. He's a talented writer — genuinely talented, the kind who makes you feel something with a sentence. He's perceptive, principled, funny when he lets his guard down, which isn't often enough. He's my closest friend here besides Katie — the guy on the other side of the wall, the one I talk to at 1 AM. I think he sees me as a good friend, maybe someone he worries about. He told me about his father's dementia after I overheard the call — the only personal thing he's ever volunteered. I know his novel is consuming him. I suspect he has feelings for Katie but he's never said it and he's never acted on it. That restraint is why I trust him.
